---
title: "randomized_svd"
generated: true
description: "Top-`k` randomised SVD $A  approx U  operatorname{diag}(s) V^{ top}$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.decompose`](../nitrix-linalg-decompose.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:randomized_svd -->
```python
randomized_svd(A: Float[Array, 'm n'], k: int, *, key: Array, n_oversample: int = 10, n_power: int = 2) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'm k'], Float[Array, 'k'], Float[Array, 'k n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:randomized_svd -->
Top-`k` randomised SVD $A \approx U \operatorname{diag}(s) V^{\top}$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:randomized_svd -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `A` | `Float` | `m` × `n` | — | `(m, n)` matrix to decompose. |
| `k` | `int` | — | — | Number of singular triplets to return (`k <= min(m, n)`). |
| `key` | `Array` | — | — | `jax.random` key for the Gaussian sketch (required -- the algorithm is randomized; the same key reproduces the result). |
| `n_oversample` | `int` | — | `10` | Oversampling $p$; the sketch width is $l = k + p$ (clamped to $\min(m, n)$). Larger $p$ improves accuracy at more cost. |
| `n_power` | `int` | — | `2` | Subspace (power) iterations $q$. Each reapplies $A A^{\top}$ (with re-orthonormalisation) to sharpen the spectral gap -- raise it for slowly decaying spectra; `0` is the plain one-pass sketch. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'm k'], Float[Array, 'k'], Float[Array, 'k n']]`. `(m, k)` orthonormal left singular vectors. `(k,)` singular values, descending and non-negative. `(k, n)` right singular vectors. `U @ jnp.diag(s) @ Vt` is the rank-`k` approximation of `A`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:randomized_svd -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
